request.question: The Jahez deck values the business at SAR 14.4 per share on a 13.31% WACC. How solid is the beta behind that WACC, and what breaks if it is wrong?

events.text:
## 1. Assessment

The beta is the single
weakest input in that WACC, and I would flag the 13.31% discount rate (and any value der
ived from it) to the Committee as provisional, not decision-grade. The thesis direction survives a beta error;

what does not survive is the margin over the benchmark.

One correction on the premise first: I c
annot find a SAR 14.4 per-share output in the connected materials. The completed Jahez analysis reports
a DCF discounted at a CAPM-derived WACC of 13.31% (4.6%
risk-free + 1.82x comp-set beta at a 5.0% equity risk premium, for a 13.7% cost of equity) with a 3.0% Gordon terminal growth
rate, an implied IRR at entry of 17.1%, and a scenario-weighted expected return of 16.8%

events.quote:
Methodology: a discounted cash flow (FCFF) model, discounted at a CAPM-derived
WACC of 13.31% (4.6% risk-free + 1.82x comp-set beta at a 5.0% equity risk premium, for a 13.7% cost
of equity) with a

METRIC VALUE
Implied IRR at entry 17.1%
Mandate hurdle rate 15.0%
Scenario-weighted expected return 16.8%
Quantified risk score (1 = lowest, 10 = highest) 5.5 / 10
Mandate fit Pass
Shariah scree

, but no per-share fair value. That isn't in my connected sources. In the MVP this routes to web, Bloomberg, or PitchBook connectors.

events.text: ## 3. What breaks if the beta is w

rong: sensitivity

Mechanically, under CAPM at a 5.0% equity risk premium, each 0.10 of beta moves the co
st of equity by 0.5%. Because the
balance sheet carries only SAR 16.6m of net cash (SAR
510.7m cash less SAR 161.2m debt and SAR 332.9m of IFRS-16 lease liabilities)[[

events.quote:
The balance sheet carries
SAR 16.6m of net cash — SAR 510.7m of cash and equivalents, less SAR 161.2m of debt and SAR
332.9m of IFRS-16 lease liabilities.

events.text: he structure is effectively all-equity, so WACC moves almost one-for-one with cost of equity; there is no debt weight

ing to dampen a beta error.

Direction (judgment): a proper bottom-up beta is more likely to come in *below* 1.82 than
above it, because unlevering Delivery Hero's leverage and re-levering to Jahez's net-cash structure would strip o
ut comp gearing that the raw 1.82x still carries. That would lower the WACC, widen the WACC-minu
s-terminal-growth spread (currently 13.31% − 3.0% = 10.31%), and push both the DCF value and the implied IRR higher.
The opposite case (a thin-liquidity or GCC/frontier risk premium argues for a beta ab

ove 2.0) raises the WACC and cuts the value.

The specific thing that breaks is the cushion

over the benchmark, not the thesis:
- The im
plied IRR of 17.1% clears the 15.0% ben
chmark by 210 basis points at entry[[13]]
, and

events.quote:
METRIC VALUE
Implied IRR at entry 17.1%
Mandate hurdle rate 15.0%
Scenario-weighted expected return 16.8%
Quantified risk score (1 = lowest, 10 = highest) 5.5 / 10
Mandate fit Pass
Shariah scree

events.text:
the scenario-weighted return clears it by ~180bps.
- A beta swing of roughly ±0.3 (we
ll inside the 1.78–1.86 comp dispersion and the uncertainty from the missing Talabat anchor) moves the WACC on
the order of ±1.4%. In a DCF with a ~10% WACC-to-terminal-growth spread, that is enough to
swing terminal value materially and to push the implied return through the 15% benchmark in the adverse

case.

Explicit flip condition: if the bottom-up beta closes out materially above ~2.0, the resulting WACC is high enough tha
t the DCF value and implied IRR likely fall below the benchmark, at which point Jahez no longer clears on a base-case ba
sis. Conversely, a bottom-up beta in the ~1.3–1.6 region would widen the cushion and make the case mo
re robust. Either way, the honest position for the Committee is that the 210bps of headroom is inside the error bar of an
unfinished WACC: the beta and cost-of-debt items in the c

omps pack should be closed before the DCF is treated as final.

Advisory only: the investment decision rests with the committee.
